title: olearia rudis var. scabra
intro: Olearia rudis var. scabra, also known as rough-hairy tree daisy, is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small white daisy-like flowers and rough, hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Olearia rudis var. scabra include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in poultices and salves to apply topically.
